title: The Slow Cup of Coffee
summary: A barista in Lisbon learns that doing one thing slowly can feel faster than doing many things at once.
body:
Mateo works in a small coffee shop in Lisbon. For two years, he tried to do everything at the same time. He made coffee, answered the phone, cleaned tables, and talked to customers all at once. He felt very busy, but he also felt tired and slow. Many customers waited a long time, and Mateo often made mistakes.

One morning, an old man named Henrique sat at the counter. He watched Mateo run from one job to the next. "You are doing five things," Henrique said quietly, "but you finish none of them." Mateo did not like these words, but he could not forget them.

The next day, Mateo tried something new. He made one coffee, and only that coffee, from start to end. He did not look at the phone. He did not think about the tables. He just watched the dark coffee fall into the white cup. It took one minute. Then he made the next coffee in the same way.

Something strange happened. The line of customers became shorter, not longer. Mateo made fewer mistakes, and people smiled more. By doing one thing slowly, he had actually become faster. "When you do many things," Henrique told him later, "your mind is never in the room. It is always in the next job." Now Mateo gives each cup his full attention. He has learned that slow, careful work is not the enemy of speed. Sometimes it is the secret of it.

annotations.phrase: run from one job to the next
annotations.meaningVi: chạy từ việc này sang việc khác
Một điều kỳ lạ đã xảy ra

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for a finished story
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để kể những việc đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The story uses past simple verbs to tell finished events, as in 'He made coffee, answered the phone, cleaned tables'.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for results now
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại hoàn thành để nói về kết quả còn ảnh hưởng đến hiện tại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Present perfect shows a result that matters now: 'He has learned that slow, careful work is not the enemy of speed.'

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ so sánh hơn thường thêm -er hoặc dùng 'more'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Comparative forms appear in 'shorter, not longer' and 'fewer mistakes'.

translationVi:
Mateo làm việc trong một quán cà phê nhỏ ở Lisbon. Trong hai năm, anh đã cố gắng làm mọi thứ cùng một lúc. Anh pha cà phê, trả lời điện thoại, lau bàn và nói chuyện với khách hàng cùng một lúc. Anh cảm thấy rất bận, nhưng anh cũng cảm thấy mệt và chậm chạp. Nhiều khách hàng phải chờ rất lâu, và Mateo thường mắc lỗi.

Một buổi sáng, một ông già tên Henrique ngồi ở quầy. Ông quan sát Mateo chạy từ việc này sang việc khác. "Cậu đang làm năm việc," Henrique nói nhẹ nhàng, "nhưng cậu không hoàn thành việc nào cả." Mateo không thích những lời này, nhưng anh không thể quên chúng.

Ngày hôm sau, Mateo thử một điều mới. Anh pha một ly cà phê, và chỉ ly cà phê đó, từ đầu đến cuối. Anh không nhìn điện thoại. Anh không nghĩ về những chiếc bàn. Anh chỉ nhìn dòng cà phê sẫm màu rơi vào chiếc cốc trắng. Việc đó mất một phút. Rồi anh pha ly tiếp theo theo cùng một cách.

Một điều kỳ lạ đã xảy ra. Hàng khách trở nên ngắn hơn, chứ không dài hơn. Mateo mắc ít lỗi hơn, và mọi người mỉm cười nhiều hơn. Bằng cách làm một việc một cách chậm rãi, anh đã thực sự trở nên nhanh hơn. "Khi cậu làm nhiều việc," Henrique nói với anh sau đó, "tâm trí cậu không bao giờ ở trong phòng. Nó luôn ở việc tiếp theo." Bây giờ Mateo dành cho mỗi ly cà phê sự chú ý hoàn toàn của mình. Anh đã học được rằng làm việc chậm rãi, cẩn thận không phải là kẻ thù của tốc độ. Đôi khi nó là bí quyết của tốc độ.

options: In a coffee shop in Lisbon
questions.explanation: The text says he 'works in a small coffee shop in Lisbon'.

questions.prompt: For two years, how did Mateo try to work?
options: Doing everything at the same time
questions.explanation: He 'tried to do everything at the same time'.

questions.prompt: How did Mateo feel while doing many jobs at once?
questions.explanation: He 'felt very busy, but he also felt tired and slow'.
questions.hint: Look at how he felt in the first paragraph.

questions.prompt: What did Henrique say to Mateo?
options: You are doing five things but you finish none of them
questions.explanation: Henrique said, 'You are doing five things, but you finish none of them.'
questions.hint: Look at the old man's words.

questions.prompt: What did Mateo do the next day?
options: He made one coffee from start to end
He cleaned all the tables first
questions.explanation: He 'made one coffee, and only that coffee, from start to end'.

questions.prompt: What happened to the line of customers?
questions.explanation: 'The line of customers became shorter, not longer.'

questions.prompt: In the sentence 'It is always in the next job', what does 'It' mean?
questions.explanation: Henrique says your 'mind is never in the room. It is always in the next job', so 'It' is the mind.
questions.hint: Look at Henrique's later words about the mind.

questions.prompt: What does 'fewer mistakes' suggest about Mateo's new way of working?
questions.explanation: Making 'fewer mistakes' shows more careful work, which the story praises.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: Coffee shops are hard to run
Doing one thing slowly can make you faster
questions.explanation: 'By doing one thing slowly, he had actually become faster.'
questions.hint: Think about the whole story, not one line.

questions.prompt: What is the tone at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: The ending reflects quietly: 'Sometimes it is the secret of it.'

questions.prompt: What does the phrase 'the enemy of speed' mean in the passage?
options: A thing that makes you slow
questions.explanation: The story says slow work is 'not the enemy of speed', meaning it does not make you slower.
questions.hint: Look at the second to last sentence.

blanks.explanation: He felt 'very busy' from doing many jobs.
He made the coffee 'from start to end'.
The coffee fell into 'the white cup'.
He gives each cup his 'full attention'.
Slow work is 'the secret of it', not the enemy.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Henrique says, "When you do many things, your mind is never in the room." Do you agree that your mind can be 'somewhere else' even when your body is busy? Describe one time this happened to you.
